questions.q: Paragraph: The loganthmic function is defined as $\log _a \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y} \Rightarrow(\mathrm{a})^y=\mathrm{x}$ where $\mathrm{x}\gt0$. $\mathrm{a}\gt0$ and $\mathrm{a} \neq 1 \log _{10} \mathrm{x}$ is called common logarithm, while $\log _e \mathrm{x}$ is called natural logarithm where c is an irrational quantity whose approximate value is nearly $2.71 \log _e x$ is written as $\ln x$. Where base of $\log$ is not written then it is assumed to be 10 . i.e., $\log$ $x$ means $\log _{10} \mathrm{X}$Question: Solution of the equation $\frac{\log (\sqrt{x+1}+1)}{\log (\sqrt[3]{x-40})}=3$, is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a$ denotes number of digits in $2^{50}, b$ denotes number of zero's after decimal and before first significant digit in $3^{-50}$ and $c$ denotes number of natural numbers have characteristic 3 with base 5 , then (Given: $\log _{10} 2=0.301$ and $\log _{10} 3=0.4771$ )Question: Number of factors of the number $a+b$ are:
questions.solution:
$a=$ Number of digits in $2^{50}=1+$ integral part of $50 \log _{10} 2=16$
$b=$ Number of zero's in $3^{-50}=\mid 1+$ integral part of $\left(-50 \log _{10} 3\right) \mid=23$
$3 \leq \log _5 N < 4$
$125 \leq N < 625 \quad \Rightarrow \quad c=500$
(i) $c-(a \times b)=500-16 \times 23=132$
(ii) $c=500 \Rightarrow$ sum of digits $=5$
(iii) $a+b=39$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
(i) $\log _{14} 63=\frac{\log _7 63}{\log _7 14}=\frac{1+\log _7 9}{\log _7 2+1}=\frac{1+\frac{2}{b c}}{1+\frac{1}{a b c}}=\frac{a b c+2 a}{a b c+1}$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& a b c=\log _2 7 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \log _2 4 < a b c < \log _2 8 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 < a b c < 3
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a$ denotes number of digits in $2^{50}, b$ denotes number of zero's after decimal and before first significant digit in $3^{-50}$ and $c$ denotes number of natural numbers have characteristic 3 with base 5 , then (Given: $\log _{10} 2=0.301$ and $\log _{10} 3=0.4771$ )Question: Sum of the digits of the number $c$ is:
questions.solution:
$a=$ Number of digits in $2^{50}=1+$ integral part of $50 \log _{10} 2=16$
$b=$ Number of zero's in $3^{-50}=\mid 1+$ integral part of $\left(-50 \log _{10} 3\right) \mid=23$
$3 \leq \log _5 N < 4$
$125 \leq N < 625 \quad \Rightarrow \quad c=500$
(i) $c-(a \times b)=500-16 \times 23=132$
(ii) $c=500 \Rightarrow$ sum of digits $=5$
(iii) $a+b=39$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The loganthmic function is defined as $\log _a \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y} \Rightarrow(\mathrm{a})^y=\mathrm{x}$ where $\mathrm{x}\gt0$. $\mathrm{a}\gt0$ and $\mathrm{a} \neq 1 \log _{10} \mathrm{x}$ is called common logarithm, while $\log _e \mathrm{x}$ is called natural logarithm where c is an irrational quantity whose approximate value is nearly $2.71 \log _e x$ is written as $\ln x$. Where base of $\log$ is not written then it is assumed to be 10 . i.e., $\log$ $x$ means $\log _{10} \mathrm{X}$Question: Solution of the equation $\left(\frac{\log x}{2}\right)^{(\log x)^2+\log x^2-2}=\log \sqrt{x}$
questions.solution:
Take log both the sides on base 10
$\therefore \log \left(\frac{\log _{10} x}{2}\right)\left\{\left(\log _{10} x\right)^2+2 \log _{10} x-2\right\}=\log \left(\frac{\log _{10} x}{2}\right)$
Let, $\log _{10} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t}$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \log \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\left(t^2+2 \mathrm{t}-2\right)=\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=2,1,-3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider $a=\log _2 3, b=\log _3 5$ and $c=\log _5 7$.Question: Which of the following is incorrect?
questions.solution:
(i) $\log _{14} 63=\frac{\log _7 63}{\log _7 14}=\frac{1+\log _7 9}{\log _7 2+1}=\frac{1+\frac{2}{b c}}{1+\frac{1}{a b c}}=\frac{a b c+2 a}{a b c+1}$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& a b c=\log _2 7 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \log _2 4 < a b c < \log _2 8 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 < a b c < 3
\end{aligned}
$$
